_metadata.status: LOCKED — written before any GPT-2-from-scratch or SAE evaluation
_metadata.methodology: Predictions derived from the Attribution Impossibility theorem and TinyStories results (Configs A/B). No GPT-2-from-scratch data has been collected.
_metadata.instructions: This file MUST NOT be modified after any evaluation script runs. The analysis script loads these predictions and compares against observed values.

P1_ppl_cv.name: Perplexity coefficient of variation < 5%
P1_ppl_cv.justification: All models use identical architecture and data. TinyStories CV was 0.7% (Config A) and 1.0% (Config B). GPT-2-small has more capacity, so convergence should be tighter.

P2_full_rho_low.name: Raw pairwise Spearman < 0.70
P2_full_rho_low.justification: S_12^12 symmetry group is larger than S_4^4 or S_8^6. More heads per layer means more permutation freedom. TinyStories: 0.565 (4H) and 0.540 (8H). With 12 heads, raw agreement should be similar or lower.

P3_ginv_rho_high.name: G-invariant Spearman > 0.80
P3_ginv_rho_high.justification: Between-orbit structure (layer importance, head-vs-MLP) is determined by architecture and data, not by seed. TinyStories: 0.972 (4H) and 0.982 (8H). GPT-2-small should show similar or higher G-invariant agreement.

P4_head_lift.name: G-invariant lift (heads-only) > 0.15
P4_head_lift.justification: The G-invariant projection captures non-trivial structure beyond random dimensionality reduction. TinyStories: lift = 0.558 (4H) and 0.553 (8H).

P5_within_flip_high.name: Within-layer flip rate > 0.40
P5_within_flip_high.justification: Theory predicts exactly 0.50 for perfectly symmetric training. TinyStories: 0.496 (4H) and 0.489 (8H). GPT-2-small with 12 heads should be indistinguishable from 0.50.

P6_between_flip_low.name: Head-vs-MLP flip rate < 0.15
P6_between_flip_low.justification: Heads and MLPs are architecturally distinct (not in the same orbit). Their relative importance should be stable across seeds. TinyStories: 0.000 at both scales.

P7_perm_test.name: Permutation test p < 0.01
P7_perm_test.justification: The G-invariant projection should be significantly better than random projections to the same dimensionality. TinyStories: p < 0.001 at both scales.

S1_feature_matching.name: Mean max cosine similarity of matched features
S1_feature_matching.justification: Bricken et al. (2023) report high reproducibility of SAE features across training runs. SAEs decompose a FIXED model's activations, so there is no Rashomon set over models — only over SAE training seeds. The decomposition should be largely determined by the activation structure.

S2_importance_stability.name: Feature importance ranking Spearman after matching
S2_importance_stability.justification: If features are well-matched (S1), their importance (activation frequency) should be similar. The importance is determined by the frozen model's activation statistics, not by the SAE seed.

S3_dead_feature_stability.name: Dead feature fraction CV across seeds < 20%
S3_dead_feature_stability.justification: The number of dead features is determined primarily by the SAE capacity and the activation distribution, not by the seed. Some variation is expected from optimization dynamics.
